entity.name: Build Up
entity.description: We transform conflict in the digital age. At the intersection of peacebuilding and technology, Build Up uses new tools for old problems, and old tools for new problems. We implement programs, conduct research, and develop technical solutions to engage with conflict and build societies in which everyone can thrive.

projects.name: Phoenix

channels.description: For substantive donations, contact us for direct bank transfer to a US bank.

plans.name: Platform Fixed Costs
plans.description: Supports ongoing costs for server hosting, database management, and infrastructure maintenance.

plans.name: Full-Time Developer Sponsorship
plans.description: Sponsor a full-time developer to contribute to the Phoenix open-source platform, ensuring continuous feature improvement, updates, and bug fixing.

plans.name: Part-Time Developer Support
plans.description: Fund part-time developer contributions to focus on specific features and enhancements.

plans.name: Scraping Credits Sponsorship
plans.description: Sponsor the (estimated) cost of data scraping for 10 peacebuilding organisations to conduct a social media listening project over one year.

plans.name: Engagement Fund
plans.description: Support trainings, manuals and onboarding activities for peacebuilders and mediators using the Phoenix platform.

plans.name: Open Donation
plans.description: For contributors who wish to donate any amount as a gesture of support towards our mission.

history.description: Funding from Build Up overhead covered the development of the code base for Phoenix v1 (see: https://gitlab.com/howtobuildup/phoenix_v1).

A 200,000 USD grant from the Swiss Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, and an additional top-up contribution from Build Up, covered the development of the Phoenix platform (which build on Phoenix v1).
A contribution from Build Up overhead will ensure continuity of the Phoenix platform in 2025, as we fundraise to cover additional costs.
